revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=新羅神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県多治見市御幸町2丁目99番地| 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] 八王子神 [[...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=新羅神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県多治見市御幸町2丁目99番地| 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] 八王子神 [[八幡神]] [[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]]| 社格=元郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=10月第2日曜日| 神事=初えびす（1月5日）| }} '''新羅神社'''（しんらじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]にある[[神社]]。
多治見市の産土神とされている。古くより厄除、災禍除に御利益があるといわれ、延命長寿、安産祈願、家庭円満の神社として信仰されている。
西之宮大神（蛭子命）が合祀されており、毎年1月5日の初[[えびす]]は賑わう。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[奈良時代]]前期、この地域（田只見郷）は[[新羅]]系の氏族が住んでいたことから、新羅系氏族の祖神を祀ったのが始まりという。[[平安時代]]の美濃神名帳の「従五位下田只見明神」と記述されている。
[[鎌倉時代]]、[[多治見氏]]により[[八幡神]]が合祀される。[[室町時代]]の[[応永]]年間には[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]が合祀されている。
現在の社殿は[[嘉永]]元年（[[1848年]]）の再建である。
== 祭神 == *[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] *八王子神 **[[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] **[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]] **[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] **[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] **[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] **[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]] **[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比売命]] **[[タキツヒメ|多岐都比売命]] *[[八幡神]] *[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]]|
== 所在地 == *岐阜県多治見市御幸町2丁目99番地
== 交通機関 == *[[中央本線]][[多治見駅]]より徒歩15分
[[Category:岐阜県の神社|しんらしんしや]] [[Category:多治見市|しんらしんしや]]
{{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=新羅神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県多治見市御幸町2丁目99番地| 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] 八王子神 [[八幡神]] [[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]]| 社格=元郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=10月第2日曜日| 神事=初えびす（1月5日）| }} '''新羅神社'''（しんらじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]にある[[神社]]。
多治見市の産土神とされている。古くより厄除、災禍除に御利益があるといわれ、延命長寿、安産祈願、家庭円満の神社として信仰されている。
西之宮大神（蛭子命）が合祀されており、毎年1月5日の初[[えびす]]は賑わう。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[奈良時代]]前期、この地域（田只見郷）は[[新羅]]系の氏族が住んでいたことから、新羅系氏族の祖神を祀ったのが始まりという。[[平安時代]]の美濃神名帳の「従五位下田只見明神」と記述されている。
[[鎌倉時代]]、[[多治見氏]]により[[八幡神]]が合祀される。[[室町時代]]の[[応永]]年間には[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]が合祀されている。
現在の社殿は[[嘉永]]元年（[[1848年]]）の再建である。
== 祭神 == *[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] *八王子神 **[[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] **[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]] **[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] **[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] **[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] **[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]] **[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比売命]] **[[タキツヒメ|多岐都比売命]] *[[八幡神]] *[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]]
== 所在地 == *岐阜県多治見市御幸町2丁目99番地
== 交通機関 == *[[中央本線]][[多治見駅]]より徒歩15分
[[Category:岐阜県の神社|しんらしんしや]] [[Category:多治見市|しんらしんしや]]
{{shinto-stub}}

現在の社殿は[[嘉永]]元年（[[1848年]]）の再建である。
== 祭神 == *[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] *八王子神 **[[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] **[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]] **[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] **[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] **[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] **[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]] **[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比売命]] **[[タキツヒメ|多岐都比売命]] *[[八幡神]] *[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]]

{{神社| 名称=新羅神社| 画像=[[File:Sinra jinja.JPG|250px]]| 所在地=岐阜県多治見市御幸町2丁目99番地| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}}| 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] 八王子神 [[八幡神]] [[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]]| 社格=元郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=10月第2日曜日| 神事=初えびす（1月5日）| }} '''新羅神社'''（しんらじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]にある[[神社]]。
多治見市の産土神とされている。古くより厄除、災禍除に御利益があるといわれ、延命長寿、安産祈願、家庭円満の神社として信仰されている。
西之宮大神（蛭子命）が合祀されており、毎年1月5日の初[[えびす]]は賑わう。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[奈良時代]]前期、この地域（田只見郷）は[[新羅]]系の氏族が住んでいたことから、新羅系氏族の祖神を祀ったのが始まりという。[[平安時代]]の美濃神名帳の「従五位下田只見明神」と記述されている。
[[鎌倉時代]]、[[多治見氏]]により[[八幡神]]が合祀される。[[室町時代]]の[[応永]]年間には[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]が合祀されている。
現在の社殿は[[嘉永]]元年（[[1848年]]）の再建である。
== 祭神 == *[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] *八王子神 **[[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] **[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]] **[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] **[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] **[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] **[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]] **[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比売命]] **[[タキツヒメ|多岐都比売命]] *[[八幡神]] *[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]]
== 文化財 == *新羅神社社殿 附陶製灯籠・棟札（市指定有形文化財 建造物）<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-12-20 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>

{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんらしんしや たしみ}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:多治見市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人|社しんらしんしや たしみ]] [[Category:多治見市の建築物]]

{{神社 | 名称=新羅神社 | 画像=[[File:Sinra jinja.JPG|250px]] | 所在地=岐阜県多治見市御幸町2丁目99番地 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] 八王子神 [[八幡神]] [[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]] | 社格=元郷社 | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=10月第2日曜日 | 神事=初えびす（1月5日） }}
'''新羅神社'''（しんらじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]にある[[神社]]。
多治見市の産土神とされている。古くより厄除、災禍除に御利益があるといわれ、延命長寿、安産祈願、家庭円満の神社として信仰されている。
西之宮大神（蛭子命）が合祀されており、毎年1月5日の初[[えびす]]は賑わう。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[奈良時代]]前期、この地域（田只見郷）は[[新羅]]系の氏族が住んでいたことから、新羅系氏族の祖神を祀ったのが始まりという。[[平安時代]]の美濃神名帳の「従五位下田只見明神」と記述されている。
[[鎌倉時代]]、[[多治見氏]]により[[八幡神]]が合祀される。[[室町時代]]の[[応永]]年間には[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]が合祀されている。
現在の社殿は[[嘉永]]元年（[[1848年]]）の再建である。
かつて、本殿の北側周辺には[[明治時代|明治]]末期から[[昭和時代|昭和]]中頃にかけて[[東濃地方]]随一の遊郭であった[[西ヶ原遊郭]]が存在した。
== 祭神 == *[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] *八王子神 **[[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] **[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]] **[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] **[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] **[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] **[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]] **[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比売命]] **[[タキツヒメ|多岐都比売命]] *[[八幡神]] *[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]]
== 文化財 == *新羅神社社殿 附陶製灯籠・棟札（市指定有形文化財 建造物）<ref>{{Cite web |date=2012-12-20 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>
== 所在地 == *岐阜県多治見市御幸町2丁目99番地
== 交通機関 == *[[中央本線]][[多治見駅]]より徒歩15分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{神社 | 名称=新羅神社 | 画像=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | 所在地=[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]御幸町2丁目99番地 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]
八王子神
[[八幡神]]
[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]] | 社格=郷社 | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=10月第2日曜日 | 神事=初えびす（1月5日） }} '''新羅神社'''（しんらじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]御幸町2丁目99番地にある[[神社]]。社格は旧郷社。 多治見村（田只味郷）の[[産土神]]とされている。古くより厄除、災禍除に御利益があるといわれ、延命長寿、安産祈願、家庭円満の神社として信仰されている。 西之宮大神（蛭子命）が合祀されており、毎年1月5日の初[[えびす]]は賑わう。 == 沿革 == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|拝殿]] 創建時期は不明。[[奈良時代]]前期、この地域（田只見郷）は[[新羅]]系の氏族が住んでいたことから、新羅系氏族の祖神を祀ったのが始まりという。[[平安時代]]の美濃神名帳の「従五位下田只見明神」と記述されている。 [[鎌倉時代]]、[[多治見氏]]により[[八幡神]]が合祀される。[[室町時代]]の[[応永]]年間には[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]が合祀されている。 現在の社殿は[[嘉永]]元年（[[1848年]]）の再建である。社殿の彫刻は尾張流御用彫物師の早瀬長兵衛の作である。文化財建築の専門家である[[名古屋工業大学]]の麓和善の調査・設計・監理によって、2012年（平成24年）と2013年（平成25年）には社殿保存修理事業を実施した。 [[明治時代|明治]]末期から[[昭和時代|昭和]]中頃にかけて、境内の北側周辺には[[東濃地方]]随一の遊郭であった[[西ヶ原遊郭]]が存在した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 *[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] ; 摂末社祭神 *八王子神 **[[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] **[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]] **[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] **[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] **[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] **[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]] **[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比売命]] **[[タキツヒメ|多岐都比売命]] *[[八幡神]] *[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]] == 文化財 == === 市指定有形文化財 === * 「新羅神社社殿 附陶製灯籠・棟札」（建造物） - 2006年（平成18年）3月10日指定{{Cite web |date=2012-12-20 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}。 == 交通アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]][[多治見駅]]より徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんらしんしや たしみ}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:多治見市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人|社しんらしんしや たしみ]] [[Category:多治見市の建築物]]

八王子神
[[八幡神]]
[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]] | 社格=郷社、[[金幣社]] | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=10月第2日曜日 | 神事=初えびす（1月5日） }} '''新羅神社'''（しんらじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]御幸町2丁目99番地にある[[神社]]。社格は旧郷社。 多治見村（田只味郷）の[[産土神]]とされている。古くより厄除、災禍除に御利益があるといわれ、延命長寿、安産祈願、家庭円満の神社として信仰されている。 西之宮大神（蛭子命）が合祀されており、毎年1月5日の初[[えびす]]は賑わう。 [[金幣社]]であり、多治見市金幣三社（'''新羅神社'''・[[本土神社]]・[[白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|白山神社]]）の一つである。 == 沿革 == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|拝殿]] 創建時期は不明。[[奈良時代]]前期、この地域（田只見郷）は[[新羅]]系の氏族が住んでいたことから、新羅系氏族の祖神を祀ったのが始まりという。[[平安時代]]の美濃神名帳の「従五位下田只見明神」と記述されている。 [[鎌倉時代]]、[[多治見氏]]により[[八幡神]]が合祀される。[[室町時代]]の[[応永]]年間には[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]が合祀されている。 現在の社殿は[[嘉永]]元年（[[1848年]]）の再建である。社殿の彫刻は尾張流御用彫物師の早瀬長兵衛の作である。文化財建築の専門家である[[名古屋工業大学]]の麓和善の調査・設計・監理によって、2012年（平成24年）と2013年（平成25年）には社殿保存修理事業を実施した。 [[明治時代|明治]]末期から[[昭和時代|昭和]]中頃にかけて、境内の北側周辺には[[東濃地方]]随一の遊郭であった[[西ヶ原遊郭]]が存在した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 *[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] ; 摂末社祭神 *八王子神 **[[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] **[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]] **[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] **[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] **[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] **[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]] **[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比売命]] **[[タキツヒメ|多岐都比売命]] *[[八幡神]] *[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]] == 文化財 == === 市指定有形文化財 === * 「新羅神社社殿 附陶製灯籠・棟札」（建造物） - 2006年（平成18年）3月10日指定{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-12-20 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}。 == 交通アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]][[多治見駅]]より徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんらしんしや たしみ}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:多治見市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人|社しんらしんしや たしみ]] [[Category:多治見市の建築物]]

]]]][[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]][[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]][[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]][[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]][[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]]]][[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]]]][[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]][[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]]]]]]]][[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]]]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]]]] {{Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | Image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | Location=[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | Coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | Deities={{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}
Hachioji no Kami
{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)}}). == Deities == ; Main Deity *{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|タキリビメ}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ}} (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsu-hime|ja|タキツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)}}). == Deities == ; Main Deity *{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagiri-hime|ja|タキリビメ}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ}} (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsu-hime|ja|タキツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]][[Category:new pages]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

[[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] {{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity={{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}
Hachioji no Kami
{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ}} (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ}} (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:New]]

In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity={{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}}
Hachioji no Kami
{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|12=draft}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|12=draft}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|12=draft}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|12=draft}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ|12=draft}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|12=draft}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|12=draft}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ|12=draft}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|12=draft}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|12=draft}} (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|12=draft}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|12=draft}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity={{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Hachioji no Kami
{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

revision.comment: 23 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:新羅神社 (多治見市) up to rev 103045631
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=新羅神社 | 画像=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | 所在地=[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]御幸町2丁目99番地 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | 祭神=[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]
八王子神
[[八幡神]]
[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]] | 社格=郷社、[[金幣社]] | 創建=不明 | 本殿= | 例祭=10月第2日曜日 | 神事=初えびす（1月5日） }} '''新羅神社'''（しんらじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[多治見市]]御幸町2丁目99番地にある[[神社]]。社格は旧[[郷社]]。 多治見村（田只味郷）の[[産土神]]とされている。古くより厄除、災禍除に御利益があるといわれ、延命長寿、安産祈願、家庭円満の神社として信仰されている。 西之宮大神（蛭子命）が合祀されており、毎年1月5日の初[[えびす]]は賑わう。 [[金幣社]]であり、多治見市金幣三社（'''新羅神社'''・[[本土神社]]・[[白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|白山神社]]）の一つである。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 *[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]] ; 摂末社祭神 *八王子神 **[[アメノオシホミミ|正勝吾勝勝速日天之忍穂耳命]] **[[アメノホヒ|天之菩卑能命]] **[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]] **[[イクツヒコネ|活津日子根命]] **[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]] **[[タキリビメ|多紀理毘売命]] **[[イチキシマヒメ|市寸島比売命]] **[[タキツヒメ|多岐都比売命]] *[[八幡神]] *[[ヒルコ|西之宮大神]] == 沿革 == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|拝殿]] 創建時期は不明。[[奈良時代]]前期、この地域（田只見郷）は[[新羅]]系の氏族が住んでいたことから、新羅系氏族の祖神を祀ったのが始まりという。[[平安時代]]の美濃神名帳の「従五位下 田只見明神」と記述されている。 [[鎌倉時代]]、[[多治見氏]]により[[八幡神]]が合祀される。[[室町時代]]の[[応永]]年間には[[スサノオ|建速須佐之男命]]が合祀されている。 現在の社殿は[[嘉永]]元年（[[1848年]]）の再建である。 社殿の彫刻は尾張流御用彫物師の早瀬長兵衛の作である。文化財建築の専門家である[[名古屋工業大学]]の麓和善の調査・設計・監理によって、 [[平成]]24年（[[2012年]]）と[[平成]]25年（[[2013年]]）には社殿保存修理事業を実施した。 [[明治時代|明治]]末期から[[昭和時代|昭和]]中頃にかけて、境内の北側周辺には[[東濃地方]]随一の遊郭であった[[西ヶ原遊郭]]が存在した。 == 文化財 == === 市指定有形文化財 === * 「新羅神社社殿 附陶製灯籠・棟札」（建造物） - 平成18年（[[2006年]]）3月10日指定{{Cite web|和書|date=2012-12-20 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}。 == 交通アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]][[多治見駅]]より徒歩15分 == 参考文献 == *『多治見市史　通史編　上』　第七章　宗教と寺社　第四節　神社　新羅神社　p792～p793　多治見市　1980年 == 脚注 == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんらしんしや たしみ}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:多治見市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人|社しんらしんしや たしみ]] [[Category:多治見市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|イチキシマヒメ|" to "|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|"
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity={{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Hachioji no Kami
{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishimahime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ichikishimahime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Tajimi, Gifu buildings and structures]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity={{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
Hachioji no Kami
{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **{{ill|Ame-no-Oshihomimi|ja|アメノオシホミミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihomimi|qq=}} (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|アメノホヒ|1=Ame no Hohi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|qq=}} (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|アマツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}} (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ikutsuhikone|ja|イクツヒコネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikutsuhikone|qq=}} (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **{{ill|Kumano-kusubi|ja|クマノクスビ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano-kusubi|qq=}} (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *{{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}} *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}} at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]]

It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:多治見市の建築物]] [[Category:多治見市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Tajimi
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity=[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
Hachioji no Kami
[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人]]

In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人]]

The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tajimi, Gifu]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity=[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
Hachioji no Kami
[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal designated tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]
