It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:渡来人]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity=[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
Hachioji no Kami
[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[Meiji period]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内のMunicipal designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:岐阜県内のMunicipal designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity=[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
Hachioji no Kami
[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[Shōwa period]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine | Name=Shinra Shrine | image=[[File:Tajimi Shinra jinja ac.jpg|280px]] | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]][[:en:Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]] Miyuki-cho 2-99 | coordinates={{coord|35|19|50.02|N|137|7|38.89|E|scale:20000}} | deity=[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
Hachioji no Kami
[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[:en:Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[:en:Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Hachioji no Kami
[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[:en:Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[:en:Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]][[Category:qq]]

Hachioji no Kami
[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]]
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) | Shrine Ranking=Gōsha, {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} | Established=Unknown | Main Hall= | Annual Festival=Second Sunday of October | Ceremonies=Hatsu Ebisu (January 5) }} '''Shinra Shrine''' (新羅神社, '''Shinra Jinja''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Miyuki-cho 2-99, [[:en:Tajimi, Gifu|Tajimi]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is regarded as the [[:en:Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=|1=Hondo-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519913}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=|1=Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496750}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. It has long been believed to grant protection from misfortune and calamity and is venerated for longevity, safe childbirth, and household harmony. The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=|1=Hondo-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519913}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=|1=Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496750}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]][[Category:qq]]

The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=|1=Hondo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519913}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496750}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E6%96%B0%E7%BE%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%A4%9A%E6%B2%BB%E8%A6%8B%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]] [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Shinra Shrine (Tajimi City)]]

It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=|1=Hondo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519913}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496750}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q123407865}}

The shrine is regarded as the [[:en:Ubusuna|guardian deity]] of Tajimi Village (Tatajimi-gō). The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q123407865}}

The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=|1=Hondo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519913}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496750}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11503945}}

It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|qq=|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|qq=|1=Hondo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519913}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|qq=|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496750}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11503945}}

The deity Nishinomiya Ōkami (Hiruko-no-Mikoto) is enshrined alongside, and the shrine is bustling every year on January 5th for the Hatsu [[Ebisu]] festival. It is a {{ill|Kinpeisha|ja|金幣社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinpeisha|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929592}} and one of the three Kinpei shrines of Tajimi City ('''Shinra Shrine''', {{ill|Hondo Shrine|ja|本土神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondo Shrine|1=Hondo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519913}}, and {{ill|Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|ja|白山神社 (多治見市白山町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine (Tajimi, Hakusan-cho)|1=Hakusan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496750}}). == Deities == ; Main deity *[[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) ; Subordinate Shrine Deities *Hachioji no Kami **[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]] (Masakatsu Agatsu Katsu Hayahi Ame no Oshihomimi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ame no Hohi|Ame no Hohi]] (Ame no Hohi no Mikoto) **[[:en:Amatsuhikone|Amatsuhikone]] (Amatsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Ikutsuhikone|Ikutsuhikone]] (Ikutsuhikone no Mikoto) **[[:en:Kumanokusubi|Kumano-kusubi]] (Kumano-kusubi no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|タキリビメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Takiribime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto) **{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|タキツヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} (Tagitsuhime no Mikoto) *[[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] *{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}} (Nishinomiya Ōkami) == History == [[File:Tajimi Shinra-jinja 2021-07 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (worship hall)]] The date of establishment is unknown. In the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], the area (Tatajimi-gō) was inhabited by a clan of [[:en:Silla|Shinra]] descent, and it is believed that the shrine was founded to enshrine their ancestral deity. The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] "Mino Jinmyōchō" (美濃神名帳) as "Jugoinoge Tatajimi Myōjin" (従五位下 田只見明神). During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the [[Tajimi clan]] enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] at the shrine. In the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], during the Ōei era, [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto) was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 1 (1848). The shrine's carvings were made by the Owari school master sculptor Hayase Chōbei. Restoration work was conducted in [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 24 (2012) and [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 25 (2013) under the investigation, design, and supervision of Nagoya Institute of Technology cultural heritage expert Kazuyoshi Fumoto. From the late [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] to the mid-[[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa era]], the area north of the shrine grounds was home to the Nishigahara Red-light district, the largest in the [[Tōnō region]]. == Cultural Properties == === City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * "Shinra Shrine Shrine Building with Ceramic Lanterns and Ridge Tags" (Building) - Designated on March 10, [[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 18 (2006){{Cite web|title=指定文化財 |publisher=多治見市 |url=http://www.city.tajimi.lg.jp/bunkazai/siteibunka/siteibunka.htm |date=2012-12-20 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}. == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:JR Central|JR]][[Chūō Main Line (Japan)|Chūō Main Line]] [[:en:Tajimi Station|Tajimi Station]] == References == * 'Tajimi City History - General Edition, Vol. 1' Chapter 7 Religion and Shrines, Section 4 Shrines, Shinra Shrine, pp. 792–793, Tajimi City, 1980 == Notes == {{Commonscat|Shinra-jinja}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|新羅神社 (多治見市)|version=103045631|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:新羅神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tajimi]] [[Category:History of Tajimi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Toraijin]] {{Commons category|Shinra-jinja}} [[ja:新羅神社 (多治見市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11503945}}
